name: Ector
description: Sir Ector, also known as Hector, is a pivotal figure in Arthurian legend, serving as Arthur's foster father. He is portrayed as a noble and virtuous knight, loyal to the crown and deeply committed to his family. Ector's significance lies primarily in his role in raising Arthur, unaware of the boy's royal lineage. He provides Arthur with a loving and stable upbringing, teaching him the values of chivalry, honor, and justice. When Arthur proves himself the rightful king by pulling the sword from the stone, Ector humbly acknowledges Arthur's destiny and swears fealty to him. Despite not being Arthur's biological father, Ector's influence is profound, shaping Arthur into the wise and just king he becomes. He represents the ideals of foster care and unconditional love, demonstrating that family is not always defined by blood. Ector's unwavering support and guidance are crucial to Arthur's early development and eventual success. He remains a respected figure in Arthur's court, embodying the virtues of loyalty, humility, and service. His son, Sir Kay, often contrasts Ector's noble character, highlighting Ector's exceptional qualities. Ector's estate is often located in the forests and fields outside of Camelot. He is a strong and capable knight, but is more well known for his good nature and wisdom.
subtitle: Arthur's foster father, noble knight.
achievements: Raising Arthur with love and care
Recognizing Arthur's right to the throne
Serving Arthur loyally throughout his reign

keyMyths.title: Arthur's Fosterage
keyMyths.description: Ector raises Arthur as his own son, unaware of the boy's true parentage. This act of kindness and devotion sets the stage for Arthur's future greatness.

keyMyths.title: The Sword in the Stone
keyMyths.description: Ector is present when Arthur draws the sword from the stone, proving his worthiness to be king. He witnesses Arthur's destiny unfold before his eyes and accepts it with grace.

keyMyths.title: Oath of Fealty
keyMyths.description: Ector kneels before Arthur and swears allegiance to him, acknowledging Arthur as his rightful king. This act solidifies Arthur's position and demonstrates Ector's unwavering loyalty.
keyMyths.source: Sir Gawain and the Green Knight

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends, featuring Ector's role in Arthur's upbringing.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends, featuring Ector's role in Arthur's upbringing.

sources.author: T.H. White
sources.description: A modern retelling of the Arthurian legend, exploring Ector's character in depth.
sources.text: A modern retelling of the Arthurian legend, exploring Ector's character in depth.
